summary: Russian forces launched a massive combined missile and drone attack on Kyiv from multiple directions including cruise missiles, ballistic missiles, jet-powered Shahed drones, and 'Banderyol' barrage munitions. The SBU struck Saky airbase in Crimea with five hits on hangars containing Su-30/SM fighters. DeepStateUA reports Russian forces gained 84 sq km of territory in June while Ukrainian energy infrastructure attacks caused power restrictions across six regions.
key_points: Massive Russian air attack on Kyiv involving cruise missiles from Bryansk via Chernihiv/Sumy, ballistic missiles from Bryansk, jet-powered Shahed drones, and 'Banderyol' barrage munitions. (Source: @kpszsu/67041, @kpszsu/67040, @mon1tor_ua/68447, @kpszsu/67072)
SBU conducted drone strike on Saky military airbase in Crimea with five hits on hangars storing Su-30 and Su-30SM fighters, causing fire in Su-30SM hangar. (Source: @UkraineNow/82307)

DeepStateUA reports Russian forces gained 84 sq km of occupied territory in June with 4.4% increase in assault actions, noting advances near Kostyantynivka, DKU, and Huliaipole. (Source: @DeepStateUA/23612)
Russian Ministry of Defense claims liberation of Ukrainskoye in Kharkiv region and Kopani in Zaporizhzhia region. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31295)

Ukraine implements electricity restrictions 17:00-22:00 due to heat and Russian attacks on energy infrastructure in Chernihiv, Kharkiv, Dnipropetrovsk, Zaporizhzhia, Kherson, and Kirovohrad regions. (Source: @UkraineNow/82312)
Ukraine signs agreement for 16 Gripen C/D fighters by early 2027 and purchase of 16 Gripen E by early 2029 with Meteor missile capability. (Source: @DeepStateUA/23611)
Ombudsman Lubinets reports mobilization commissions declaring seriously ill individuals (diabetes, hypertension, HIV, syphilis, sepsis) fit for service. (Source: @UkraineNow/82313, @UkraineNow/82314)

Russian jet-powered drones entered Chernihiv region from Bryansk; multiple Shahed drones over Kyiv districts including Obolon, Vinogradar, Minsk massif, Shulyavka, Solomianka, Podil. (Source: @eRadarrua/99215, @mon1tor_ua/68447, @mon1tor_ua/68449, @mon1tor_ua/68448)

sections.executive_summary: Russian forces launched a massive combined missile and drone attack on Kyiv from multiple directions including cruise missiles, ballistic missiles, jet-powered Shahed drones, and 'Banderyol' barrage munitions. The SBU struck Saky airbase in Crimea with five hits on hangars containing Su-30/SM fighters. DeepStateUA reports Russian forces gained 84 sq km of territory in June while Ukrainian energy infrastructure attacks caused power restrictions across six regions.

points: SBU executed precision drone strike on Saky airbase in Crimea with five confirmed hits on hangars housing Su-30 and Su-30SM fighters, causing fire in Su-30SM hangar; each aircraft valued at $30-50 million. (Source: @UkraineNow/82307)

Ukraine signed historic agreement with Sweden for 16 Gripen C/D fighters arriving early 2027 as military aid, plus purchase of 16 Gripen E fighters by early 2029 with Meteor missile capability for long-range air targets. (Source: @DeepStateUA/23611)

Ukrenergo imposed electricity consumption restrictions 17:00-22:00 nationwide due to high demand from heatwave and Russian attacks damaging energy infrastructure in six regions: Chernihiv, Kharkiv, Dnipropetrovsk, Zaporizhzhia, Kherson, Kirovohrad. (Source: @UkraineNow/82312)

Ombudsman Lubinets exposed mobilization medical commissions declaring individuals with severe comorbidities (diabetes, hypertension, obesity, psoriasis; HIV, syphilis, hepatitis B/C, sepsis) as fully fit for service, with hospitalized cases within days. (Source: @UkraineNow/82313, @UkraineNow/82314)

Ukrainian air defense engaged multiple threat streams simultaneously: cruise missiles over Brovary and Desna, ballistic missiles from Bryansk, jet-powered Shahed drones over Kyiv districts, and 'Banderyol' barrage munition from eastern direction. (Source: @eRadarrua/99228, @kpszsu/67041, @mon1tor_ua/68447, @kpszsu/67072)

Russian forces launched coordinated multi-axis attack on Kyiv: cruise missiles from Bryansk region through Chernihiv and Sumy regions, ballistic missiles from Bryansk, jet-powered Shahed drones from Bryansk/Kursk directions, and 'Banderyol' barrage munition from eastern vector. (Source: @kpszsu/67041, @kpszsu/67040, @eRadarrua/99215, @kpszsu/67072)
Russian Ministry of Defense claims liberation of Ukrainskoye settlement in Kharkiv region and Kopani in Zaporizhzhia region, per Russian MoD report. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31295)
Russian missile strike on Zaporizhzhia region directed toward Kryvyi Rih; ballistic missile threat reported from occupied Crimea (Iskander-M). (Source: @kpszsu/67049, @mon1tor_ua/68442)
Russian assault actions increased 4.4% in June with territorial gain of 84 sq km, including advances near Kostyantynivka, DKU, and Huliaipole per DeepStateUA analysis. (Source: @DeepStateUA/23612)

Russian air defense shot down four drones over Leningrad region per governor report; Ukrainian drone with 5kg explosive crashed in Turkish Trabzon province. (Source: @tass_agency/383268, @tass_agency/383238)

DeepStateUA reports Russian territorial gain of 84 sq km in June with advances near Kostyantynivka, DKU (Donetsk direction), and Huliaipole (Zaporizhzhia direction) despite increased assault actions rising 4.4%. (Source: @DeepStateUA/23612)
Russian MoD claims liberation of Ukrainskoye in Kharkiv region and Kopani in Zaporizhzhia region, indicating continued pressure on eastern and southeastern fronts. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31295)
Russian missile strike on Zaporizhzhia region toward Kryvyi Rih demonstrates continued targeting of Dnipropetrovsk region logistics and infrastructure. (Source: @kpszsu/67049)
DeepStateUA notes Russian assault quality remains low with frequent single-soldier attacks, but quantity increased; Ukrainian successes not yet visible on map for June. (Source: @DeepStateUA/23612)

Multiple Russian cruise missile groups launched from Bryansk region through Chernihiv and Sumy regions toward Kyiv: missiles over Brovary, Desna, Snivska area toward Chernihiv/Ripky, and Konotop area toward Chernihiv/Kyiv. (Source: @eRadarrua/99228, @eRadarrua/99218, @eRadarrua/99235, @mon1tor_ua/68433)
Russian ballistic missiles launched from Bryansk region through Chernihiv region toward Kyiv; at least two separate launches reported. (Source: @kpszsu/67041, @kpszsu/67040)

Jet-powered Shahed drones entered Chernihiv region from Bryansk toward Slavutych; additional drones over Poltava region (Hlobyne, Myrhorod, Pyriatyn) and Chernihiv region along border. (Source: @eRadarrua/99215, @eRadarrua/99255)
Russian drone groups moving toward Kyiv from northern direction per air defense monitoring. (Source: @kpszsu/67019)

SBU drone strike on Saky military airbase in Crimea with five hits on hangars containing Su-30 and Su-30SM fighters; fire confirmed in Su-30SM hangar indicating successful target destruction. (Source: @UkraineNow/82307)
Russian ballistic missile threat (Iskander-M) reported from occupied Crimea toward Ukrainian territory. (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68442)
Russian strike drones operating in northern Mykolaiv region moving in northern direction. (Source: @kpszsu/67022)
Russian MoD claims liberation of Kopani in Zaporizhzhia region, suggesting incremental gains in southern operational zone. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31295)

Massive combined Russian air attack on Kyiv: cruise missiles from multiple vectors (Brovary, Desna, Snivska, Konotop), ballistic missiles from Bryansk, jet-powered Shahed drones over Kyiv districts (Obolon, Vinogradar, Minsk massif, Shulyavka, Solomianka, Podil, Troieshchyna), and 'Banderyol' barrage munition from eastern direction. (Source: @eRadarrua/99228, @kpszsu/67041, @mon1tor_ua/68447, @kpszsu/67072)

SBU executed successful long-range drone strike on Saky airbase in Crimea hitting five hangars with Su-30/SM fighters, demonstrating Ukrainian deep-strike capability against high-value Russian aviation assets. (Source: @UkraineNow/82307)

Ukraine finalized Gripen fighter agreement: 16 C/D variants by early 2027 as Swedish military aid, 16 E variants by early 2029 purchased via Eurocredit with UK support, including Meteor beyond-visual-range missiles. (Source: @DeepStateUA/23611)

Russian jet-powered Shahed drones (реактивні шахеди) deployed from Bryansk/Kursk directions, representing technological upgrade from propeller-driven variants with higher speed and reduced reaction time. (Source: @eRadarrua/99215, @mon1tor_ua/68447, @mon1tor_ua/68449)

Ukrainian air defense engaged simultaneous threats across northern, eastern, and southern approaches to Kyiv, including cruise missiles over Zhurivka toward Kyiv and new group from Sumy region. (Source: @eRadarrua/99235, @kpszsu/67061, @kpszsu/67063)

key_developments: SBU struck Saky airbase in Crimea with 5 drone hits on hangars containing Su-30/SM fighters, causing fire in Su-30SM hangar; each jet worth $30-50M. (Source: @UkraineNow/82307)

Russia launched massive multi-wave attack on Kyiv: cruise missiles from Bryansk via Chernihiv/Sumy, ballistic missiles from Bryansk, jet-powered Shahed drones over 7 Kyiv districts, and 'Banderyol' barrage munition from east. (Source: @kpszsu/67041, @mon1tor_ua/68447, @kpszsu/67072)

DeepStateUA: Russian forces gained 84 sq km in June with 4.4% more assault actions; advances near Kostyantynivka, DKU, Huliaipole; Ukrainian counter-gains not yet visible. (Source: @DeepStateUA/23612)

Russian MoD claims liberation of Ukrainskoye (Kharkiv) and Kopani (Zaporizhzhia); Ukrainian energy infrastructure attacks caused blackouts in 6 regions prompting 17:00-22:00 restrictions. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31295, @UkraineNow/82312)
Ukraine signed Gripen deal: 16 C/D by early 2027 as Swedish aid, 16 E by early 2029 with Meteor missiles; pilots training in Sweden. (Source: @DeepStateUA/23611)

Ombudsman Lubinets: Mobilization commissions declaring severely ill (diabetes, HIV, sepsis, etc.) fit for service; man with broken ribs held 18 days in TCC until bones healed. (Source: @UkraineNow/82313, @UkraineNow/82314)

Russian jet-powered Shahed drones entered Chernihiv from Bryansk; cruise missiles over Brovary, Desna, Snivska, Konotop; ballistic missiles from Bryansk; drones over Poltava, Mykolaiv. (Source: @eRadarrua/99215, @eRadarrua/99228, @kpszsu/67041, @kpszsu/67022)
sections.threat_assessment: Russia's coordinated multi-axis attack on Kyiv demonstrates improved integration of cruise missiles, ballistic missiles, jet-powered Shahed drones, and new 'Banderyol' barrage munitions to saturate Ukrainian air defenses. The simultaneous SBU strike on Saky airbase destroying high-value Su-30SM fighters shows Ukraine's growing deep-strike capability against Russian aviation sanctuaries. Russian territorial gains of 84 sq km in June with increased assault tempo near Kostyantynivka and Huliaipole suggest continued offensive pressure despite low-quality attacks. Energy infrastructure targeting across six regions indicates systematic degradation of Ukrainian power grid ahead of winter. The Gripen acquisition with Meteor missiles will significantly enhance Ukrainian air defense range but operational deployment remains years away.
sections.regional_response: Poland refuses to relinquish European Peace Fund compensation claims despite Kyiv's request for €6.6B transfer. Polish President Nawrocki stripped Zelenskyy of Order of White Eagle over UPA commemoration and national pantheon legislation, escalating historical memory disputes. Turkey reported Ukrainian drone crash in Trabzon province with 5kg explosive, highlighting spillover risks. Russia and Tanzania agreed on direct flights, showing Moscow's continued diplomatic outreach to Global South. UN faces budget crisis as US withholds regular contributions, potentially affecting humanitarian operations in Ukraine.
sections.intelligence_notes: Russian deployment of jet-powered Shahed drones from Bryansk/Kursk reduces early warning time for Kyiv air defenses due to higher speed and lower altitude profiles. 'Banderyol' barrage munition appearance suggests Russian adaptation of loitering munition swarm tactics. SBU's precise targeting of specific hangars at Saky indicates actionable intelligence on Russian fighter dispersal patterns. Mobilization of medically unfit personnel reflects Russian attrition pressure on Ukrainian manpower but risks combat effectiveness and domestic stability. DeepStateUA's assessment of 'turning point' in war aligns with observed Russian territorial gains versus Ukrainian deep-strike successes, suggesting strategic competition between Russian ground pressure and Ukrainian asymmetric capabilities.
